one who carries sacred images in procession
to be in a state of
rehearsing a comedy, training the chorus
iron pole round which grain is piled in conical shape,
produce by means of conic sections
a light semi-transparent garment, made at Cos
pieces of wood fit for making oars, spars
of any similar motion forwards and backwards
leathern sack hung up for punching
